Class RsaKeyPair

java.lang.Object
com.seq.acheron.secrets.asymmetric.AbstractAsymmetricKeyPair
com.seq.acheron.secrets.asymmetric.RsaKeyPair
public class RsaKeyPair extends AbstractAsymmetricKeyPair
RSA implementation of AbstractAsymmetricKeyPair.

Provides factory methods to generate and reconstruct RSA key pairs.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    RsaKeyPair(PublicKey publicKey, PrivateKey privateKey)
    Creates a new RsaKeyPair from existing key objects.

  • Constructor Details

    • RsaKeyPair

      public RsaKeyPair(PublicKey publicKey, PrivateKey privateKey)
      Creates a new RsaKeyPair from existing key objects.
      Parameters:
      publicKey - RSA public key
      privateKey - RSA private key

  • Method Details

    • generate

      public static RsaKeyPair generate(int keySize) throws NoSuchAlgorithmException
      Generates a new RSA key pair with the given key size (in bits).

      Common sizes are 2048 or 3072 bits; 4096 bits can be used for long-term security. [web:132][web:134]

      Parameters:
      keySize - the RSA modulus size in bits (e.g. 2048)
      Returns:
      a new RsaKeyPair instance
      Throws:
      NoSuchAlgorithmException - if RSA is not supported

    • fromBase64

      public static RsaKeyPair fromBase64(String base64PublicKey, String base64PrivateKey) throws NoSuchAlgorithmException, InvalidKeySpecException
      Reconstructs an RsaKeyPair from Base64-encoded public and private keys.

      Expects:

      • Public key in X.509 / SubjectPublicKeyInfo format
      • Private key in PKCS#8 format
      Parameters:
      base64PublicKey - Base64-encoded RSA public key
      base64PrivateKey - Base64-encoded RSA private key
      Returns:
      the reconstructed RsaKeyPair
      Throws:
      NoSuchAlgorithmException - if RSA is not supported
      InvalidKeySpecException - if key data is malformed

    • getAlgorithm

      public String getAlgorithm()
      Description copied from class: AbstractAsymmetricKeyPair
      Returns the algorithm name (e.g. "RSA", "EC") for this key pair.
      Specified by:
      getAlgorithm in class AbstractAsymmetricKeyPair
      Returns:
      the algorithm identifier
